checked_dimensions.name: spec_compliance
checked_dimensions.evidence: Issue #261 authorized a bounded v3 repository build, private GitHub repository creation, normal non-force push, local tests, read-only CI, minimalism review gate, and v1 evidence files. The final report records those outcomes.

checked_dimensions.name: acceptance_criteria
checked_dimensions.evidence: The v3 repo exists locally and remotely as private. It contains README, AGENTS, VERSION, validator, scripts, tests, docs, context, contract/scope/review/readiness/rollback artifacts, a read-only CI workflow, local tests passing, and CI run 28203941848 passing on commit 83ddcfa0358cb302ea11aadf0bdb0fc38f241f71.

checked_dimensions.name: minimalism_gate
checked_dimensions.evidence: Codex hardened the Claude-generated gate from five freeform strings into structured review evidence: review_id, scope_id match, minimal_change_rationale, reuse_inventory array, new_files_justification objects, dependency_policy none/empty, validation_plan array, overbuild_risks array, and verdict pass. Negative tests cover array shape, missing reason, dependency declaration, non-pass verdict, scope mismatch, and parent traversal.

checked_dimensions.name: policy_boundaries
checked_dimensions.evidence: The work stayed inside declared v1 evidence files and the declared external v3 repository path. It did not access secrets, install dependencies, deploy, change production, billing, auth, authorization, database, external AI providers, hosted runtime, MCP mutation, public visibility, force push, or branch deletion.

checked_dimensions.name: security_rules
checked_dimensions.evidence: No credentials, keys, production settings, auth, billing, database, deployment, or destructive infrastructure were touched. The only remote mutation was the authorized private repo creation and normal non-force push to v3 origin/master after local tests passed.

checked_dimensions.name: changed_file_scope
checked_dimensions.evidence: All v1 evidence files are declared in the issue #261 scope manifest. The v3 changed files are inside D:\Antigravity\Infinite Process\specbridge-v3, which is explicitly allowed by the contract.

checked_dimensions.name: test_evidence
checked_dimensions.evidence: SpecBridge v3 local tests passed with bootstrap.tests.ps1 reporting 177 passed, 0 failed and minimalism-review.tests.ps1 reporting 24 passed, 0 failed.

checked_dimensions.name: ci_evidence
checked_dimensions.evidence: GitHub Actions run 28203941848 for v3 workflow CI completed successfully on 83ddcfa0358cb302ea11aadf0bdb0fc38f241f71. The v1 evidence branch has not been opened as a PR, so v1 merge CI is not collected.

checked_dimensions.name: final_report_honesty
checked_dimensions.evidence: The final report records the Claude-first bootstrap, the second Claude timeout, Codex hardening and review, successful local tests, successful private repo creation, successful push, successful CI, the v1 merge gate, and residual risks.

findings.evidence: The v1 evidence branch has not been opened as a PR and therefore has no GitHub CI or review gate for merge into main.
findings.recommendation: Open a v1 PR for the evidence branch and merge only after required gates pass and operator merge authority is explicit.

findings.evidence: The v3 repository was created and CI passed, but branch protection was not configured because branch-protection mutation is outside this contract.
findings.recommendation: Configure branch protection under a future dedicated repository-governance contract if v3 becomes a long-lived product repository.

findings.evidence: The v3 MVP validates contracts, scopes, and minimalism review evidence but does not enforce runtime operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement runtime operation enforcement under a future dedicated contract if v3 moves beyond static validation.

unresolved_risks: V1 evidence is not merged into main.
Runtime operation enforcement remains future scope.
External Claude/provider orchestration remains future scope.
CI action SHA pinning remains future CI workflow hardening scope.
